text:
British judoka Chelsie Giles’ hopes of making the Olympic podium again were ended by a golden score defeat to Brazil’s Larissa Pimenta.

Giles, bronze medallist in Tokyo three years ago, went out in the last 16 of the -52 kg category much to her obvious disappointment.

In a tense encounter, Giles was on the backfoot after picking up a couple of penalties for non-combative activity.

The judokas could not be separated in the four minutes of regulation time so the match went to golden score extra time and it was the Brazilian who struck first to claim victory.

Speaking to the BBC afterewards, a distraught Giles said: ‘You put in a lot of work and it can be done in a second.

‘That’s how judo works, you only get once chance. So that’s it. Once it gets to golden score it’s just about getting that score.

“You put in a lot of work and it can be done in a second, that’s how judo works, you only get one chance” ðGB’s Chelsie Giles was knocked out in the second round of the women’s -52kg judo in Paris.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/Hyd0OzJtQ4

‘I thought I was getting a bit better in the fight and I just got caught so there’s not much you can do.’

Asked if she would take comfort from the fact here family were in attendance, having been forced to travel alone to Tokyo three years ago due to Covid restrictions, a tearful Giles said: ‘Yes, I’m grateful my family could be here so I just thank them.’

Giles then preceded to pull away from the microphone, leaving the BBC’s reporter to thank her for her efforts and outline his hope that she would be back to compete in Los Angles in 2028.

In a subsequent interview, Giles confessed she left to count the cost of making a slow start, saying:’It was just a very tactical game, I’ve fought her several times before.

‘It started off a bit slow and I picked up two shidos.

‘When it went into golden score (sudden death overtime), I thought I was doing a bit better.

‘Then I just got unlucky and got caught off the wrong side. There’s not much I could have done, I just got caught.’

title: Heartbroken Chelsie Giles cuts BBC interview short after Olympics loss | Metro News
description: Team GB Judo star Chelsie Giles - who has previously won Bronze - faced a heartbreaking defeat, causing the BBC to cut her interview short.
